title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal risk; however, if improperly configured or exploited, it could expose sensitive dependency data or provide attackers with insights into unpatched vulnerabilities.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and security practices before implementation to ensure it aligns with organizational security policies.
Run the tool in a controlled environment and avoid exposing sensitive project details to external systems.
Monitor for updates or patches to the tool itself, as it may gain attention from malicious actors due to its popularity.

title: Bandit trusts client-supplied URI scheme on plaintext connections
description:
### Summary
Bandit reflects the client-supplied URI scheme into `conn.scheme` without verifying the actual transport. Over a plaintext HTTP/1.1 connection (or h2c), an unauthenticated attacker can send an absolute-form request target like `GET https://victim/path HTTP/1.1` and the application observes `conn.scheme = :https` even though no TLS was negotiated. Any downstream Plug logic that trusts `conn.scheme` as a security signal — `Plug.SSL`'s "already secure, don't redirect" branch, `secure: t

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39807.

title: Bandit is vulnerable to CL.CL request smuggling via unrejected duplicate `Content-Length` header
description:
### Summary

Bandit is vulnerable to CL.CL HTTP request smuggling: it silently accepts requests with two `Content-Length` headers whose values differ, takes the first value, and dispatches the body bytes as a second pipelined request on the same keep-alive connection. RFC 9110 §5.3 prohibits multiple lines for singleton fields like `Content-Length`, and RFC 9112 §6.3 item 5 requires the recipient to treat invalid `Content-Length` as an unrecoverable framing error. When Bandit sits behind a proxy

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows HTTP request smuggling attacks, which can lead to unauthorized access, data leakage, or manipulation of backend systems. Attackers could exploit this to bypass security controls, perform cache poisoning, or execute malicious requests.
recommended_actions: Update Bandit to the latest patched version that resolves CVE-2026-39805, ensuring compliance with RFC standards for handling `Content-Length` headers.
Implement strict input validation and rejection of malformed HTTP requests at the proxy or load balancer level to mitigate the risk of request smuggling.
Conduct a security audit of your HTTP handling infrastructure to identify and address potential vulnerabilities related to request parsing.

title: Bandit Buffers Unbounded WebSocket Continuation Frames, Allowing Unauthenticated Memory Exhaustion
description:
### Summary
A single unauthenticated WebSocket client can exhaust server memory in any Bandit-fronted application that accepts WebSocket connections. The fragmented-message reassembly path appends every `Continuation{fin: false}` frame's payload to a per-connection iolist with no cumulative size cap, so a peer that streams continuation frames indefinitely (never setting `fin=1`) grows BEAM heap linearly until the OS or a supervisor kills the process. `max_frame_size` only bounds individual frame

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42786.

title: Bandit's unbounded WebSocket inflate causes BEAM OOM with a single frame
description:
### Summary

When a Bandit-fronted server has explicitly enabled WebSocket permessage-deflate (`compress: true`), an unauthenticated client can OOM the BEAM with a single ~6 MiB WebSocket frame. Bandit's inflate step has no output-size cap, so a small high-ratio compressed frame (e.g. zeros, ~1024:1 ratio) decompresses unbounded into the connection process before any application code runs. Phoenix and LiveView are **not** vulnerable by default — they ship with `compress: false`. Affected apps ar

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39804.

title: gittuf's policy can be rolled back to prior valid versions
description:
## Summary

An attacker with push access to gittuf's Reference State Log (RSL) can roll back the current policy to any previous policy trusted by the current set of root keys.

## Impact

gittuf determines the policy to load by inspecting the RSL. Except for the very first policy (which is automatically trusted given gittuf's TOFU model, or verified against manually specified keys), whenever an RSL entry that points to a new policy is encountered, gittuf validates that this policy is trusted. Th

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: An attacker with push access to gittuf's Reference State Log (RSL) can exploit the rollback vulnerability to enforce outdated or insecure policies, potentially bypassing security measures and introducing vulnerabilities into the system.
recommended_actions: Implement stricter access controls and monitoring for push access to the Reference State Log (RSL) to prevent unauthorized changes.
Introduce version pinning or forward-only policy validation to ensure policies cannot be rolled back to older versions.
Regularly audit and validate the integrity of the RSL to detect and respond to unauthorized modifications promptly.

title: The Forminator Forms plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in versions up...
description: The Forminator Forms plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in versions up to and including 1.51.1. This is due to the `processRequest()` method in `Forminator_Admin_Module_Edit_Page` (admin/abstracts/class-admin-module-edit-page.php) dispatching sensitive module-management actions — including export, delete, clone, delete-entries, publish/draft, and bulk variants — after only a nonce check, without ever verifying that the current user holds the `manage_forminator_modules` c

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Forminator Forms plugin allows unauthorized users to perform sensitive actions such as exporting, deleting, cloning, or publishing forms. This could lead to data breaches, loss of critical information, or unauthorized modifications to website content.
recommended_actions: Update the Forminator Forms plugin to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict access to the WordPress admin panel to trusted users only and monitor for unusual activity.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block unauthorized actions targeting the plugin.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44597.

title: ZTE ZX297520V3 BootROM contains a vulnerability that allows arbitrary memory writes via USB....
description: ZTE ZX297520V3 BootROM contains a vulnerability that allows arbitrary memory writes via USB. Attackers can exploit the lack of target address validation in the USB download mode to write data to any location in BootROM runtime memory, thereby overwriting the stack, hijacking the execution flow, bypassing the Secure Boot signature verification mechanism, and achieving unauthorized code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40003.

title: The Appointment Booking Calendar plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in...
description: The Appointment Booking Calendar plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in versions up to and including 1.6.10.6. This is due to a flawed authorization logic in the nonce_permissions_check() method combined with the public exposure of a site-wide reusable nonce. The plugin exposes a public_nonce value through the /wp-json/ssa/v1/embed-inner endpoint, which is accessible to unauthenticated users. The appointment deletion endpoint at /wp-json/ssa/v1/appointments/{id}/delete an

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated users to exploit a missing authorization flaw, potentially enabling them to delete appointments without proper permissions. This could disrupt business operations, lead to data integrity issues, and damage customer trust.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Appointment Booking Calendar plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily if no update is provided.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to block unauthorized access to the vulnerable endpoints.
Audit and monitor appointment-related activity logs for any unauthorized deletions or suspicious behavior.

title: FileBrowser Vulnerable to Stored XSS via SVG File in Public Share (Missing CSP Header)
description:
## Summary

FileBrowser Quantum serves inline SVG files without a `Content-Security-Policy` header, allowing embedded JavaScript in SVG files to execute when accessed via public share links.

Verified on v1.3.0-stable.

## Affected product

- **Product:** FileBrowser Quantum (`gtsteffaniak/filebrowser`)
- **Verified version:** v1.3.0-stable
- **Docker image:** gtstef/filebrowser:latest
- **Affected endpoint:** `GET /public/api/resources/download?hash=HASH&inline=true`
- **CWE:** CWE-79 — Cross-s

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: FileBrowser Public Share DELETE API Path Traversal Allows Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Deletion
description:
### **Summary**

Attacker-controlled path input is joined with a trusted base path prior to sanitization, allowing traversal sequences (e.g., ../) to escape the intended shared directory. As a result, an unauthenticated attacker possessing a valid public share hash with delete permissions enabled can delete arbitrary files outside the shared directory within the share owner’s configured storage scope.

### **Affected Components**

**Two distinct vulnerable code paths:**

1. Stable versions (e.g.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to delete arbitrary files outside the intended shared directory, potentially causing data loss, service disruption, and compromising system integrity.
recommended_actions: Immediately update FileBrowser to a patched version that addresses CVE-2026-44542.
Disable public share functionality or restrict delete permissions until a fix is applied.
Implement strict access controls and monitor logs for unauthorized file deletion activity.

title: etcd RBAC bypass allows unauthorized data access via PrevKv/lease attachment in nested transaction Put requests
description:
### Impact
_What kind of vulnerability is it? Who is impacted?_

A vulnerability in etcd allows read access via PrevKv, or lease attachment in Put requests within transaction operations, to bypass RBAC authorization checks. An authenticated user without sufficient read or lease-related permissions may be able to access unauthorized data or attach leases by invoking transaction operations with these features enabled.

Kubernetes does not rely on etcd’s built-in authentication and authorization. I

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44283.

title: docling-graph has SSRF via Missing Internal IP Validation in URLInputHandler
description:
### Impact

The `URLInputHandler` class in `docling_graph/core/input/handlers.py` makes HTTP requests to user-supplied URLs without validating whether the target resolves to a private, loopback, or link-local IP address. The `URLValidator` only checks for a valid scheme and non-empty `netloc`, performing no IP-level validation. Additionally, `requests.head()` was called with `allow_redirects=True`, allowing an attacker to redirect requests to internal endpoints via an intermediary URL.

An attac

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44520.

title: imageproc: integer overflow in kernel size check leads to out-of-bounds read
description:
A bounds verification of a slice storage of a 2-dimensional matrix's coefficients (a kernel) would compare the total size against the product of individual dimensions. This would erroneously cast *after* the multiplication and consequently fail to detect possible violations when overflow occurs.

Afterwards, the individual sizes were trusted to properly constrain coordinates within the matrix to indices valid for the underlying storage. With a crafted `Kernel` object, certain combinations of coo

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to exploit an integer overflow during kernel size checks, leading to out-of-bounds reads. This can result in potential information disclosure, application crashes, or undefined behavior, depending on the context in which the vulnerable library is used.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest patched version of the 'imageproc' library, if available, to address the vulnerability.
Implement input validation to ensure kernel dimensions and sizes are within safe bounds before processing.
Conduct a security review of dependent applications to identify potential exposure to this vulnerability.

title: imageproc: Out-of-bounds read via NaN coordinates in bilinear/bicubic sampling
description:
A bounds check was performed in floating points before a cast to the index passed to an unchecked access function. This checked considered `NaN` cases improperly, causing them to succeed the check instead of failing it. The floating point coordinate is under caller control by passing a selected projection matrix.

Carefully controlling the coordinates of an image with no data and one non-zero dimension provides an arbitrary read primitive in the first 32-bits of address space with a Bilinear sam

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: imageproc has fragile bounds check when sampling from image
description: A read of pixels was coded as modifying coordinates to lie within the image bounds. It would calculate a coordinate by adding a constant to an input and taking the minimum of the resulting coordinate and 'dimension - 1'. This would not protect against malicious inputs that could overflow the addition. Following the tricked bounds check, the image could then be sampled at multiple differently calculated coordinates that exceeded the bounds.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the imageproc library's bounds checking mechanism could allow attackers to exploit out-of-bounds memory reads. This may lead to information disclosure, application crashes, or potential remote code execution if combined with other vulnerabilities.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of the imageproc library once a patch is released to address the bounds checking vulnerability.
Implement input validation and sanitization to prevent malicious inputs from triggering overflow conditions.
Conduct a security review of all dependencies and libraries in use to identify and mitigate similar vulnerabilities.

title: ShellHub has cross-tenant IDOR in `GET /api/namespaces/:tenant` via API Key bypasses  membership check
description:
## Summary
`GET /api/namespaces/:tenant` returns the full namespace object — including the members list (user IDs, e-mails, roles), settings, and device counts — to any caller authenticated by an **API Key**, for any tenant, regardless of the API Key's own tenant scope.

The handler conditionally skips the membership check when the user ID (`X-ID`) is absent, which is exactly the case for API Key authentication.

## Affected versions
ShellHub Community v0.24.1 (validated).

## Root cause

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44426.

title: hickory-proto vulnerable to CPU exhaustion during message encoding due to O(n²) name compression
description:
During message encoding, `hickory-proto`'s `BinEncoder` stores pointers to labels that are candidates for name compression in a `Vec<(usize, Vec<u8>)>`. The name compression logic then searches for matches with a linear scan.

A malicious message with many records can both introduce many candidate labels, and invoke this linear scan many times. This can amplify CPU exhaustion in DoS attacks.

This is similar to [CVE-2024-8508](https://www.nlnetlabs.nl/downloads/unbound/CVE-2024-8508.txt).

### R

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: CVE-2026-42511 - The BOOTP file field is written to the lease file without escaping embedded double-quotes, allowing
description:
The BOOTP file field is written to the lease file without escaping embedded double-quotes, allowing injection of arbitrary dhclient.conf directives.  When the lease file is subsequently re-parsed by dhclient, e.g., after a system restart, an attacker-controlled field from the lease is passed to dhclient-script(8), which evaluates it.

A rogue DHCP server may be able to execute arbirary code as root on a system running dhclient.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a rogue DHCP server to inject arbitrary dhclient.conf directives, potentially leading to remote code execution as root. This could compromise system integrity, enable privilege escalation, and facilitate lateral movement within a network.
recommended_actions: Apply patches or updates provided by your operating system vendor to mitigate CVE-2026-42511.
Configure network environments to restrict unauthorized DHCP servers and implement DHCP snooping where possible.
Monitor and audit DHCP-related logs for unusual activity or rogue server attempts.

title: CVE-2026-41226 - Open redirect vulnerability exists in Multiple laser printers and MFPs which implement Ricoh Web Ima
description: Open redirect vulnerability exists in Multiple laser printers and MFPs which implement Ricoh Web Image Monitor. When accessing a specially crafted URL, the user may be redirected to an arbitrary website. As a result, the user may become a victim of a phishing attack.

title: CVE-2024-39847 - Unauthenticated attackers can exploit a weakness in the XML parser functionality of the SOAP endpoin
description: Unauthenticated attackers can exploit a weakness in the XML parser functionality of the SOAP endpoints in 4D server. This allows them to obtain read access to files on the application server and adjacent network shares, and perform HTTP GET requests to arbitrary services.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Exploitation of this vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to access sensitive files on the application server and adjacent network shares, potentially leading to data breaches and unauthorized access to internal services.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update provided by the vendor to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict network segmentation to limit access to SOAP endpoints and adjacent network shares.
Monitor and log all SOAP endpoint activity for signs of unauthorized access or exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-7446 - A vulnerability was detected in VetCoders mcp-server-semgrep 1.0.0. This affects the function analyz
description: A vulnerability was detected in VetCoders mcp-server-semgrep 1.0.0. This affects the function analyze_results/filter_results/export_results/compare_results/scan_directory/create_rule of the file src/index.ts of the component MCP Interface. The manipulation of the argument ID results in os command injection. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. Upgrading to version 1.0.1 is able to mitigate this issue. The patch is identified as 141335da044e53c3f5b315e0386e01238405b771. It is advisable to upgrade the affected component.
